metadata.title: Definitive Paper-Ready Consolidation: JRN Hypothesis Scorecard & 8 Paper Tables
metadata.description: Consolidation of 16 experiments across 4 datasets into 8 paper tables + hypothesis scorecard

examples.input: {"claim_id": "a", "claim_description": "JRN Validity: JRN estimated from probes correlates with actual join utility"}
{"claim_id": "b", "claim_description": "Probe Cost Efficiency: Cheap probes achieve similar rankings at fraction of cost"}
{"claim_id": "c", "claim_description": "Inverted-U Threshold: Aggregation sensitivity peaks near JRN \u2248 1"}
{"claim_id": "d", "claim_description": "Multiplicative Compounding: Chain JRN \u2248 product of individual JRNs"}
{"claim_id": "e", "claim_description": "JRN-Guided Architecture: JRN-guided outperforms uniform on 60%+ tasks"}
{"claim_id": "f", "claim_description": "Training-Free Estimation: Cheap proxies can estimate JRN without training"}

examples.output: {"strong_claims": ["JRN is a valid per-join diagnostic (rho > 0.6 across 4 datasets, Grade A/B)", "JRN probes are cost-efficient (5-11% cost for 96-99% performance, Grade A)", "Mutual information is a reliable training-free proxy (rho ~ 0.75, Grade B)", "JRN decomposes into structural and feature components (Cohen's d ~ 0.66 pooled)"], "disclaimers": ["Inverted-U threshold prediction: NOT confirmed (Grade D)", "Multiplicative compounding: dataset-dependent, not universal (Grade C)", "Architecture superiority: near-oracle but not reliably better than uniform-rich (Grade C)"]}
